levels.1: This is a Python Flask blog application with registration, login, posts, feedback, and a /get_flag route. Explore authenticated and unauthenticated routes on port 8111.
levels.2: The interesting surface is POST /save_feedback after login. It accepts JSON and recursively merges user-supplied fields into a Feedback object before writing feedback to disk.
levels.3: Look for Python class/prototype pollution. The merge helper follows nested dictionaries into object attributes such as __class__, __init__, and __globals__ rather than restricting keys to normal feedback fields.
levels.4: End-to-end chain: register and log in, then POST JSON to /save_feedback with the normal feedback keys plus a nested __class__.__init__.__globals__ object that changes the module-level flag variable to the string true. After the merge, request /get_flag with the same authenticated session and submit the exact DUCTF flag returned by the service.
